content.problemStatement:
Om has a string SSS consisting of characters ∗*∗, 444, and 000 only.


The character ∗*∗ can be replaced by either 444 or 000.
Om wants to count the total number of subsequences of 404404404 present in all the possible strings generated after replacing ∗*∗.

As the number can be huge, you must output the number modulo 109+710^{9}+7109+7.

For example, if the given string is S=S =S= 4∗4∗4*4*4∗4∗, the possible strings after replacing ∗*∗ are:


404040404040: Only one subsequence of 404404404 is present, that is S1S2S3S_1S_2S_3S1​S2​S3​.
404440444044: Two subsequences of 404404404 are present, that are S1S2S3S_1S_2S_3S1​S2​S3​ and S1S2S4S_1S_2S_4S1​S2​S4​.
444044404440: No subsequence of 404404404 is present.
444444444444: No subsequence of 404404404 is present.
Thus, total number of 404404404 subsequences present in all possible generated strings is 333.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — size of the given string.
The second line has a string SSS of size NNN.




Output Format
For each test case, output the total number of subsequences of 404404404 present in all the possible strings generated after replacing ∗*∗.

As the number can be huge, you must output the number modulo 109+710^{9}+7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10610^{6}106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: All possible generated strings of 4∗044*044∗04 are:

400440044004: Two subsequences of 404404404 are present, that are S1S2S4S_1S_2S_4S1​S2​S4​ and S1S3S4S_1S_3S_4S1​S3​S4​.
440444044404: Two subsequences of 404404404 are present, that are S1S3S4S_1S_3S_4S1​S3​S4​ and S2S3S4S_2S_3S_4S2​S3​S4​.

Thus, the total number of 404404404 subsequences present in all possible generated strings is 444.
Test case 222: Already explained in problem statement.
